hadiths.chapter: 40. WHAT CAN AND CANNOT BE USED FOR
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} that He said:  
"It is disliked (makruh) because it may cause harm to the person next to him."  
[AL SADUQ] 
It is disliked for a man to wipe the dust from his forehead while in prayer, and it is also 
disliked to leave it there after the prayer. However, if a person wipes the dust from his 
forehead while in prayer, there is no issue, as leniency has been reported  regarding 
this matter. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 40, H. 842
reference.chapter: WHAT CAN AND CANNOT BE USED FOR
reference.location: Volume 1 › WHAT CAN AND CANNOT BE USED FOR

hadiths.chapter: 41. THE REASON FOR THE PROHIBITION OF
hadiths.en:
Hisham ibn al-Hakam said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}:  
"Tell me about what is permissible to prostrate on and what is not."  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Prostration is only permissible on the earth or what grows from the 
earth, except for what is eaten or worn."  
Hisham then asked: "May I be your ransom, what is the reason for that?"  
The Imam said: "Prostration is an act of humility before Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and 
Majestic, and it should not be on what is eaten or worn because the people of the world 
are slaves to what they eat and wear.  
The one who prostrates in worship of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, should not 
place his forehead on what is worshipped by the people of the world who are deceived 
by its allure. Prostration on the earth is superior because it is more effective in 
demonstrating humility and submission to Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 272 • Ilal al-Sharai’ 
Vol. 2, p. 341 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 82, p. 147

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 41, H. 843
reference.chapter: THE REASON FOR THE PROHIBITION OF
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE REASON FOR THE PROHIBITION OF

hadiths.ar:
845   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ إَّلْمُّق َّص  َّلُّ ن ْن ُّ عُّمَّزَّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ِ إَّلي  َّحْزِي ف ِ لِأ َّصْحَّان ِن َّا ذ َّإب َّ إَّلْي َّسَّارِ عَّن ِ إَّلْق ِن ْلَّه ِ وَّ عَّن ِ 
إَّلس َّن َّث ِ ق ِي هِ ق َّق َّال صَّاب ُّ إَّلْحَّزَّمِ مِن ْ جَّي ْث ُّ لَّحِق َّهُّ 
إَّلن  ُّورُّ ن ُّورُّ  إَّلْحَّح َّزِ ق َّهُّوَّ عَّن ْ ي َّمِي ن ِ إَّلْكَّعْن َّه ِ إ َّرْن َّعَّه ُّ إ َّمْي َّالٍ وَّ عَّن ْ ي َّسَّارِهَّا ي َّمَّان ِي َّه ُّ إ َّمْي َّالٍ كُّل ُّهُّ إِي ْن َّا عَّ س َّزَّ مِي لاً ق َّا ِذ َّإ إِن ْحَّزَّف َّ 
إَّلْأ ِن ْسَّان ُّ ذ َّإب َّ إَّلْي َّمِي ن ِ ج َّزَّح َّ عَّن ْ جَّذِّ إَّلْق ِن ْلَّه ِ لِق ِل َّه ِ إ َّن ْصَّاب ِ إَّلْحَّزَّمِ وَّ إ ِ ذ َّإ إِن ْحَّزَّف َّ إَّلْأ ِن ْسَّان ُّ ذ َّإب َّ إَّلْي َّسَّارِ لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ ج َّارِج اً عَّن ْ 
جَّذِّ إَّلْق ِن ْلَّه ِ .    
ومن  كان  ق ى  إلمسح ذ إلحزإم صلى إ لى إلكعن ه  إ لى إ ى  ج وإن ن ها س اء، ومصلى ق ى  إلكعن ه  صلى إ لى إ ى  ج وإن ن ها س اء، وإ ق ص ل 
ذ لك  إ ن  ي ق ف  ن ي ن  إلعموذي ن  على لن لاطه  إلحمزإء ، وي سي ق ن ل إلزكن  إلذ ى  ق ي ه إلحح ز إلأ سوذ، ومن  كان  ق وق  إلكعن ه  وجص زب   
إلصلاة  إص طح غ وإ وما  ن زإ سه إ لى إلن ي ث  إلمعمور  ، ومن  كاف  ق وق  إ ن ى ق ن ي س إسي ق ن ل إلكعن ه  وصلى ق ان  إلكعن ه  ما ق وق ها إ لى  
إلسماء. وصلى رسول إلله صلى إلله علي ه وإ له إ لى إلن ي ث  إلمق ذس ن عذ إلن ن وة  ي لاب  عس زة  سن ه  ن مكه  وي سعه  عس ز س هزإ  
ن المذي ن ه ، ي م عي زي ه إلي هوذ ق ق الوإ له: إ ن ك  ي ان غ لق ن لي ن ا ق اع ي م لذ لك  ع ما س ذي ذإ ق لما كان  ق ى   ن عض  إللي ل ج زح  صلى إلله علي ه 
وإ له وسلم ي ق لث  وج هه ق ى  إ ق اق  إلسماء ق لما إ صن ح صلى إلع ذإة ، ق لما صلى من  إلط هز ركعي ي ن  ج اءة ج ن زن ي ل علي ه إلسلام 
ق ق ال له:  " ق ذ ن زى ي ق لث  وج هك  ق ى  إلسماء ق لن ولي ن ك  ق ن له  ي زص اها ق ول وج هك  س طز إلمسح ذ إلحزإم إلأ ي ه  " ي م إ ج ذ  ن ي ذ  
إلن ن ى  صلى إلله علي ه وإ له ق حول وج هه إ لى إلكعن ه  وجول من  ج لق ه وج وههم جي ى ق ام إلزج ال مق ام إلن ساء وإلن ساء مق ام 
إلزج ال ق كان  إ ول صلاي ه إ لى ن ي ث  إلمق ذس وإ ج زها إ لى إلكعن ه  ، ون لغ  إلح ن ز مسح ذإ ن المذي ن ه  وق ذ صلى إ هله من  إلعصز 
ركعي ي ن  ق حولوإ ن حو إلكعن ه ، ق كان ث  إ ول صلاي هم إ لى ن ي ث  إلمق ذس وإ ج ز ها إ لى إلكعن ه  ق سمى  ذ لك  إلمسح ذ مسح ذ إلق ن لي ي ن   
ق ق ال إلمسلمون : صلاي ن ا إ لى ن ي ث  -  إلمق ذس ي ص ي غ ي ا رسول إلله؟ ق ا ن ز ل إلله عز  وج ل " وما كان  إلله لي ص ي غ إ ي مان كم " ي عن ى   
صلاي كم إ لى ن ي ث  إلمق ذس، وق ذ إ ج زج ث  إلح ن ز ق ى  ذ لك  على وج هه ق ى  كي اب  إلن ن وة .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said :  
"Indeed, Allah {SWT}, Blessed and Exalted, made the Ka’ba the Qiblah (direction of 
prayer) for the people of the mosque (Masjid al-Haram), and He made the mosque the 
Qiblah for the people of the sanctuary (Haram), and He made the sanctuary the Qiblah 
for the people of the world." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 272 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 2, p. 195 • Ilal 
al-Sharai’ Vol. 2, p. 415 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 44 • Awaali al -La’aali, Part 3, p. 71 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 7, 
p. 541 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 303 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 304 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 81, p. 50 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 42, H. 844

hadiths.ar:
846  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ عَّن ْذِ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ :  إ َّن  َّهُّ سَّا َّلَّ إَّلص َّاذِق َّ عَّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ إ َّعْمَّى صَّل َّى عَّلَّى ع َّي ْزِ إَّلْق ِن ْلَّه ِ ق َّق َّالَّ  
« هِى َّ مُّي َّع َّي ِّمَّه   ي ُّم 
ي َّح َّل َّث ْ ق َّ عَّلِمَّ إ َّن  َّهُّ صَّل َّى عَّلَّى ع َّي ْزِ إَّلْق ِن ْلَّه ِ ق َّق َّال  ق َّلاَّ ي ُّعِي ذُّ .
hadiths.en:
Al-Mufaddal ibn Umar asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about 
the deviation of some of our companions to the left from the Qiblah and the reason for 
it.  
Imam {a.s} replied: "When the Black Stone (Hajar al -Aswad) was brought down from 
Paradise and placed in its position, the boundaries of the Haram (sanctuary) were 
determined by the reach of the light emanating from the Stone. It is four miles to the 
right of the Ka’ba and eight miles to the left, making a total of twelve miles.  
Thus, if a person deviates to the right, they leave the boundary of the Qiblah due to the 
lesser extent of the Haram's boundary, but if they deviate to the left, they do not leave 
the boundary of the Qiblah." 
[AL SADUQ] 
For those within Masjid al-Haram, it is permissible to face any side of the Ka’ba during 
prayer. One praying inside the Ka’ba may also face any of its walls. However, the best 
position is to stand between the two columns on the red tile and face the corner  
containing the Black Stone. If someone is above the Ka’ba and it is time for prayer, they 
should lie down and incline their head toward the Bayt al -Ma'mur (Heavenly House). 
Similarly, anyone praying on Mount Abu Qubays should face the Ka’ba, as the Qiblah  
extends upward to the sky. 
The Prophet {saws} prayed toward Bayt al -Maqdis (Jerusalem) for thirteen years in 
Makkah after receiving prophethood and for nineteen months in Madinah.  
The Jews mocked him, saying that he was following their Qiblah, causing him great 
distress. One night, he turned his face toward the heavens in anticipation, and in the 
morning, after completing two units (rak'ahs) of the Dhuhr prayer, Archangel Jibril {a.s} 
came to him and said:  
"We have seen you turning your face toward the sky, so We shall turn you to a Qiblah 
that pleases you. Turn your face toward the Sacred Mosque (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:144)."  
Archangel Jibril {a.s} then took the Prophet’s hand and turned him toward the Ka’ba, and 
those behind him also turned, so that men and women changed their positions.  
Thus, his initial prayer was toward Bayt al -Maqdis, and the remainder was toward the 
Ka’ba. 
The news reached a mosque in Madinah where people had already prayed two rak'ahs 
of 'Asr. They turned toward the Ka’ba, and so their initial prayer direction was toward 
Bayt al-Maqdis and its completion was toward the Ka’ba. This mosque became known 
as Masjid al-Qiblatayn (the Mosque of the Two Qiblahs).  
The Muslims then asked:  
"O’ Messenger of Allah {saws}, what happens to our prayers toward Bayt al-Maqdis?"  
Allah {SWT} revealed: " Allah {SWT} would not make your faith to be in vain  (Surah Al -
Baqarah 2:143)," meaning that their prayers toward Bayt al -Maqdis were accepted. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 272 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 44 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 42, H. 845
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER OF THE QIBLA
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ٨٤٥

hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Abdul Rahman ibn Abi Abdullah that he asked Imam Jafar 
Al-Sadiq {a.s} about a blind man who prayed facing a direction other than the Qiblah.  
Imam {a.s} replied: "If it is still within the prayer time, he should repeat the prayer. If the 
time has passed, he does not need to repeat it."  
He further asked about a man who prayed during cloudy weather and afterward, when 
the clouds cleared, realized he had prayed in the wrong direction.  
The Imam said: "If it is still within the prayer time, he should repeat the prayer. If the 
time has passed, he does not need to repeat it”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol 1, p. 276 • Al-Wafi, V. 7, p. 551 • Wasail Al-Shia, V. 4, p. 318 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 42, H. 846
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER OF THE QIBLA
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ٨٤٦

hadiths.ar:
848   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ مُّعَّاوِي َّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّم َّارٍ :   عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّق ُّومُّ ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ي ُّم َّ ي َّن ْط ُّزُّ ن َّعْذَّ مَّا ق َّزَّع َّ ق َّي َّزَّى إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّذِ إِن ْحَّزَّف َّ عَّن ِ إَّلْق ِن ْلَّه ِ 
ي َّمِي ن اً إ َّوْ س ِمَّالأً ق َّق َّالَّ لَّه .   
ون ز لث  هذ ة ق ى  ق ن له  إلمي حي ز " ولله إلمس زق  وإلمع زب  ق ا ي ن ما ي ولوإ ق ي م وج ه إلله .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah and Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Al -Baqir {a.s} that He 
said: "For one who is confused (about the direction of the Qiblah), it suffices for him to 
pray in whichever direction he faces if he does not know where the Qiblah is."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, V 1, p. 276 • Al-Wafi, V. 7, p. 548 • Wasail Al-Shia, V. 4, p. 311 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 42, H. 847
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER OF THE QIBLA
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ٨٤٧

hadiths.ar:
849   -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  جَّمْز َّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلْأ َّو َّلِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال لن  َّز  ُّ مِن ْ ج َّلْف ِ إَّلْكَّن ِي ف ِ 
وَّ هُّوَّ ق ِى  إَّلْق ِن ْلَّه ِ ي َّسْي ُّزُّةُّ ن ِس َّى ْءٍ .    
ولأ ي ق طغ صلاة  إلمسلم س ى  ي مز ن ي ن  ي ذي ه من  كلث  إ و إمزإ ة  إ و جمار إ و ع ي ز ذ لك  .
hadiths.en:
Muawiya ibn Ammar asked Him about a man who stands for prayer and, after 
finishing, realizes that he had deviated to the right or the left of the Qiblah.  
Imam {a.s} replied: "His prayer is valid, for the area between the east and the west is 
(considered) the Qiblah." 
[AL SADUQ] 
And this was revealed concerning those who are confused about the direction:  
"To Allah {SWT} belongs the east and the west; wherever you turn, there is the face of 
Allah {SWT}" (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:115)  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Volume 1, p. 276 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 48 • Al-Istibsar, 
Part 1, p. 297 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 554 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 314 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 42, H. 848
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER OF THE QIBLA
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ٨٤٨

hadiths.ar: 850 - وَّ ن َّهَّى رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ، عَّن ِ إَّلْن ُّز َّإق ِ ق ِى  إَّلْق ِن ْلَّه ِ .
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Abi Hamzah narrated from Imam Abu al -Hasan al-Awwal {a.s} 
that He said:  
"If a seepage (liquid or discharge) appears behind a latrine (toilet) and it is in the 
direction of the Qiblah, one should cover it with something."  
[AL SADUQ] 
Nothing that passes in front of a Muslim while he is praying  -  whether it be a dog, a 
woman, a donkey, or anything else  -  invalidates the prayer.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Volume 1, p. 277 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 457 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 
4, p. 319 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 146 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 42, H. 849
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER OF THE QIBLA
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ٨٤٩

hadiths.ar:
851   -  وَّ رَّإ َّى ض ن ُّح َّامَّه ً ق ِى  إَّلْمَّسْح ِذِ ق َّمَّس َّى إ ِلَّي ْهَّا ن ِعُّزْج ُّون ٍ مِن ْ عَّزَّإج ِي ن ِ إِن ْن ِ طَّاب ٍ ق َّحَّك َّهَّا ي ُّم َّ رَّج َّغَّ إَّلْ ق َّهْق َّزَّى ق َّن َّن َّى  
عَّلَّى صَّلاَّي ِهِ وَّق َّالَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم .
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} forbade spitting in the direction of the Qiblah.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Volume 1, p. 277 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 500 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 
4, p. 319 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 42, H. 850
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER OF THE QIBLA
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ٨٥٠

hadiths.ar:
852   -  وَّ ن َّهَّى صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ عَّن ِ إَّلْح ِمَّاعِ مُّسْي َّق ْن ِلَّ إَّلْق ِن ْلَّه ِ وَّ مُّسْي َّذْن ِزَّهَّا وَّ ن َّهَّى عَّن ِ إِسْي ِ ق ْن َّالِ إَّلْق ِن ْلَّه ِ ن ِن َّوْلٍ إ َّوْ 
ع َّان ِظٍ .
hadiths.en:
The Prophet {saws} saw phlegm (or a spittle) in the mosque. He walked over to it 
with a palm branch from the branches of Ibn Tab and scraped it off, then returned 
backward and continued his prayer. 
And Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said: "This opens many doors regarding 
(permissible) actions during prayer."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Volume 1, p. 277 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 500 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 42, H. 851
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER OF THE QIBLA
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ٨٥١

hadiths.en:
The Prophet, forbade engaging in sexual intercourse while facing or with one's 
back to the Qiblah.  
He also forbade facing the Qiblah when urinating or defecating.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 42, H. 852
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER OF THE QIBLA
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ٨٥٢

hadiths.ar:
854  - ق َّالَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  «
إَّلْمَّمَّاب ِ.   
وق ذ روى ق ي من  لأ ي هي ذى  إ لى إلق ن له  ق ى  مق ار ة  إ ن ه ي صلى  إ لى إ رن غ ج وإن ث  .
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"None of you should spit in front of themselves or to their right while praying. Instead, 
they should spit to their left or beneath their left foot."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Volume 1, p. 277 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 3, p. 257 • Al -Wafi, 
Vol. 7, p. 501 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 319 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 222 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 365 
• Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 81, p. 201 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 42, H. 853
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER OF THE QIBLA
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ٨٥٣

hadiths.ar:
855 - وَّ رَّوَّى ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ث ُّ وَّ إ َّي ْن َّ جَّذ ُّ إَّلْق ِن ْلَّه ِ ق َّالَّ  
«ي ْمٍ ق ِى  ع َّي ْزِ إَّلْوَّق ْث ِ ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Whoever holds back their saliva out of reverence for Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and 
Majestic, during prayer, Allah {SWT} will grant them health until their death." 
[AL SADUQ] 
It has also been narrated regarding someone who cannot determine the Qiblah in a 
desert or wilderness that they should pray in all four directions.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 278 • Thawab al-A’mal wa Iqab al-A’mal, Part 1, p. 29 • 
Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 319 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 7, p. 265 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 81, p. 239 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 42, H. 854
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER OF THE QIBLA
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ٨٥٤

hadiths.ar:
856  -  وَّق َّال ق ِى  جَّذِي ث ٍ إ ج َّزَّ ذ َّكَّزَّةُّ لَّهُّ :  «
ق َّا ِن  َّ إَّلل َّهَّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ي َّق ُّولُّ لِن َّن ِي ِّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ق ِى  إَّلْق َّزِي ص َّه ِ زَّ إَّلْمَّسْح ِذِ إَّ لْحَّزٰإمِ وَّ جَّي ْث ُّ مٰا  
كُّن ْي ُّمْ ق َّوَّل ُّوإ وُّج ُّوهَّكُّمْ س َّطْزَّة   ق َّق ُّمْ مُّن ْي َّصِن اً 
صَّلاَّة َّ لَّهُّ وَّ إِج ْس َّغْ ن ِن َّصَّزِك َّ لِل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ وَّ لأَّ ي َّزْق َّعْ هُّ إ ِلَّى إَّلس َّمَّاءِ وَّ لْي َّكُّن ْ جِذ َّإءَّ وَّج ْهِك َّ ق ِى  مَّوْص ِغِ سُّح ُّوذِك َّ .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} that He said:  
"There is no prayer except toward the Qiblah."  
Zurarah asked: "And where is the boundary of the Qiblah?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "The entire area between the east and the west is the Qiblah."  
Zurarah then asked: "What if someone prays facing a direction other than the Qiblah, 
or on a cloudy day when they miss the correct time?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "They must repeat the prayer”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 278 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 540 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 
312 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 1, p. 137 • Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 2, p. 186 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 42, H. 855
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER OF THE QIBLA
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ٨٥٥

hadiths.ar:
857 - وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ لِز ُّرَّإرَّة َّ : « .   
وق ال إ ن ى  رص ى  إلله عن ه ق ى  رسالي ه إ لى : إ ذ إ إ رذب  إ ن  ي صلى  ن اق له  وإ ن ث  رإكث  ق صلها، وإسي ق ن ل ن زإ س ذإن ي ك  جي ث  ي وج هث 
ن ك  مسي ق ن ل إلق ن له  ومسي ذن زها وي مي ن ا وي سارإ، ق ان  صلي ث  ق زي ص ه  على ط هز ذإن ي ك  ق اسي ق ن ل إلق ن له  وكن ز ي كن ي زة  إلأق ي ي اح  
ي م إمض  جي ث  ي وج هث  ن ك  ذإن ي ك  وإق زإ ، ق ا ذ إ إ رذب   إلزكوع وإلسح وذ ق اركغ وإسح ذ على س ى  ي كون  معك  مما ي ح ور  علي ه 
إلسح وذ ولأ ي صلها   إ لأ على جال إص طزإر س ذي ذ وي ق عل ق ي ها إ ذ إ صلي ث  ماس ي ا مي ل ذ لك  إ لأ إ ن ك  إ ذ إ إ رذب  إلسح وذ سح ذب   
على إلأ رض .  وق ال ق ي ها : إ ذ إ ي عزض  لك  سن غ وج ق ث  ق وب  إلصلاة  ق اسي ق ن ل إلق ن له  وصل صلاي ك  ن الأي ماء، وإ ن  ج س  ي ث  إلسن غ  
وي عزض  لك  ق ذر معه كي ف  ذإر وصل ن الأي ماء.
hadiths.en:
In another narration, the Imam said :  
"Then turn your face toward the Qiblah and do not turn your face away from the Qiblah, 
lest your prayer become invalid. Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, says to His 
Prophet in the obligatory prayer:  
'Turn your face toward the Sacred Mosque  (Surah Al -Baqarah 2:144); and wherever 
you are, turn your faces toward it.' Stand upright."  
Prophet {saws} said: "Whoever does not straighten their spine (while standing) has no 
prayer."  
And lower your gaze in humility before Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic; do not raise 
it to the sky. Let your gaze be directed in front of you toward your place of prostration”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 278 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 312 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 42, H. 856
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER OF THE QIBLA
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ٨٥٦

hadiths.ar:
858  -  وَّ رُّوِى صَّل َّى إ ِلَّى صَّذْرِ 
إَّلس َّق ِي ن َّه ِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said to Zurarah:  
"The prayer is not to be repeated except for five things: purification (taharah), time 
(observing the prayer time), facing the Qiblah, bowing (ruku'), and prostration 
(Sujood)”. 
[AL SADUQ] 
My father, may Allah {SWT} be pleased with him, said in his letter to me:  
"If you want to perform a supererogatory (nafl) prayer while riding, pray and turn the 
head of your mount in whatever direction it faces, whether toward the Qiblah or 
otherwise, right or left. If you pray a mandatory (fardh) prayer while on the back of your 
mount, face the Qiblah, say the opening takbir (Allahu Akbar), and then proceed in 
whichever direction your mount takes you, reciting. When you wish to bow and 
prostrate, bow and prostrate on something you have with you that is permissible to 
prostrate upon. Do not pray a mandatory prayer in this manner unless there is a severe 
necessity. If you are walking, follow the same procedure, but when you wish to 
prostrate, you must prostrate on the ground." 
The letter also stated : "If a wild animal approaches and you fear missing the prayer, 
face the Qiblah and pray using gestures. If you fear the animal and it continues to circle 
you, move with it as it moves and pray using gestures." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 279 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 1, p. 371 • Wasail Al-Shia, 
Vol. 4, p. 312 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 313 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 389 • Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 2, 
p. 89 • Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 2, p. 101 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 236 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 42, H. 857
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER OF THE QIBLA
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ٨٥٧

hadiths.ar:
859 - وَّق َّال إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ : « .   
ي عن ى  ق ى  إلح معه  وإلعي ذي ن  وصلاة  إلأسي سق اء ق ى  إلح طن ه  ي سي ق ن لهم إلأمام و ي سي ق ن لون ه جي ى ي ق زع  من  ج طن ي ه
hadiths.en:
It is narrated that "If the wind blows strongly upon someone on a ship, and they 
are unable to turn toward the Qiblah, they should pray facing the front of the ship”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 280 • Al-Wafi, V. 7, p. 528 • Wasail Al-Shia, V. 4, p. 321 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 42, H. 858
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER OF THE QIBLA
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ٨٥٨

hadiths.ar:
860   -  وَّق َّال رَّج ُّل  لِلص َّاذِق ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  إ ِن ِّى  إ َّكُّون ُّ ق ِى  إَّلس َّق َّزِ وَّ لأَّ إ َّهْي َّذِى  إ ِلَّى إَّلْق ِن ْلَّه ِ ن ِالل َّي ْلِ ق َّق َّال 
إَّل َّذ ِى  ي ُّق َّالُّ لَّهُّ إَّلْح َّذْى اج ْعَّلْهُّ ن َّي ْن َّ كَّ ي ِق َّي ْك َّ .
hadiths.en:
The Prophet {saws}, said:  
"Every preacher is a Qiblah, and every one being preached to is a Qiblah for the 
preacher." 
[AL SADUQ] 
This means that during the Jumu’ah sermon (Khutbah), the Eid prayers, and the prayer 
for rain (Salat al-Istisqa), the Imam (preacher) faces the people, and they face him until 
he completes his sermon. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 42, H. 859
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER OF THE QIBLA
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ٨٥٩

hadiths.en:
A man said to Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s}:  
"I am sometimes traveling and cannot determine the Qiblah at night."  
The Imam asked: "Do you know the star that is called 'al -Jady' (the North Star)?"  
The man replied: "Yes."  
The Imam said: "Place it to your right, and if you are on the pilgrimage route, place it 
between your shoulders”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 280 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 
7, p. 547 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 306

hadiths.chapter: 43. THE AGE AT WHICH CHILDREN ARE REQUIRED TO
hadiths.ar:
862  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ِ إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ ن ْن ِ ق َّارِن ٍ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّوْ سُّن ِلَّ وَّ إ َّن َّا إ َّسْمَّغُّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ 
ي َّح ْي ِن ُّ وَّلَّذَّةُّ وَّ هُّوَّ لأَّ ي ُّصَّلِّى  إَّلْي َّوْمَّ وَّ إَّلْي َّوْمَّي ْن ِ ق َّق َّال ى َّ سِن ِي ن َّ ق َّق َّال 
ي َّي ْ زُّك ُّ إَّلص َّلاَّة
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"We command our children to pray when they are five years old, so you should instruct 
your children to pray when they are seven years old.  
We also command our children to fast when they are seven years old, as much as they 
can bear of fasting for the day  -  whether it is until midday, more, or less. If thirst or 
hunger overwhelms them, they may break their fast so that they can gradually get  
accustomed to fasting and be able to bear it. So, instruct your children to fast when 
they are nine years old, as much as they are capable of fasting for the day. If thirst 
overwhelms them, they may break their fast." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 43, H. 861
